title: Project Creation Taxonomy Fix — MCP & Multi-Tenant Safety

instructions.for_agents: Read handovers/HANDOVER_INSTRUCTIONS.md first. Then read handovers/0837_PROJECT_CREATION_TAXONOMY_FIX.md for full technical scope. Work on your assigned sub-handover. When done, update YOUR section below with: status, commit hashes, files changed, tests passing, and any issues found. Do NOT modify other agents' sections.

kickoff.0837a: You are on branch feature/0837-taxonomy-fix. Implement auto-assign series_number in project_service.py create_project(). When series_number is None, query MAX(series_number)+1 with FOR UPDATE lock scoped to tenant+type. This is the critical blocker — MCP create_project fails for any tenant with 1+ projects due to uq_project_taxonomy NULLS NOT DISTINCT. Write tests: multiple projects without taxonomy, concurrent creation, explicit taxonomy still works, soft-deleted project reuse. Read the 0837 handover for code snippets.
kickoff.0837b: You are on branch feature/0837-taxonomy-fix. Depends on 0837a being complete. Add optional 'type' parameter to create_project MCP tool. Accept human-readable label ('Frontend' not UUID). Add get_project_type_by_label() to project_service.py (case-insensitive lookup). Update MCP schema in mcp_http.py. If type not found, create without type (no error). Read the 0837 handover for code snippets and test requirements.
kickoff.0837c: You are on branch feature/0837-taxonomy-fix. Depends on 0837b being complete. Update /gil_add slash command templates in slash_command_templates.py (GIL_ADD_MD, GIL_ADD_GEMINI_TOML, GIL_ADD_CODEX_SKILL_MD) to pass the 'type' parameter when creating projects. The project creation section of each template should ask users for an optional project type. Read the 0837 handover for scope.
kickoff.0837d: You are on branch feature/0837-taxonomy-fix. Can run in parallel with 0837a-c. Audit ALL UniqueConstraints in src/giljo_mcp/models/ for multi-tenant lockout patterns. Check: NULL columns in constrained fields, missing tenant_key scoping, race conditions under concurrent writes. The 0837 handover has a table of 12 constraints to verify. Deliverable: audit report in this JSON confirming each is safe or flagging fixes needed.

instructions.closeout: When your sub-handover is complete: set status to 'complete', list all commit hashes, list files changed, confirm tests passing count and lint status. If you found issues in other agents' scope, note them in cross_cutting_findings.

0837b.agent: Type Resolution by Label + MCP Schema
fixes: Added get_project_type_by_label() to ProjectService (case-insensitive lookup by tenant)
Added project_type parameter to ToolAccessor.create_project() — resolves label to project_type_id
Added project_type to MCP schema inputSchema and _TOOL_SCHEMA_PARAMS allowlist
Used 'project_type' instead of 'type' to avoid shadowing Python builtin (ruff A002)
0837b.notes: MCP schema uses 'project_type' (not 'type') as the property name to avoid Python builtin shadow. 0837c templates should use project_type as the parameter name. Non-existent type labels silently create without type (no error). 4 unit tests + 5 integration tests added.

0837c.lint: clean (1 pre-existing Q000 in test file, not from this change)
fixes: Added --type flag to project flags in GIL_ADD_MD (Claude Code template)
Added --type flag to project flags in GIL_ADD_GEMINI_TOML (Gemini CLI template)
Added --type flag to project flags in GIL_ADD_CODEX_SKILL_MD (Codex CLI template)
Updated Direct Project Mode in all 3 templates to pass project_type to create_project MCP tool
Updated Interactive Mode project path in Claude template to ask for optional project type
Updated Interactive Mode in Gemini and Codex templates to include project type prompt
Added graceful fallback note: unrecognized type labels create project without type (no error)
Updated MCP Tool Parameters note in Claude template to document project_type param
Added Important Notes about project_type param in Gemini and Codex templates
0837c.notes: Uses 'project_type' as MCP parameter name per 0837b (avoids Python builtin shadow). All 3 templates (Claude, Gemini, Codex) consistently support --type flag for project creation. 7 new tests added to TestProjectTypeParameter class covering all platforms.

audit_results.detail: NULLS NOT DISTINCT causes lockout when MCP creates 2+ projects without taxonomy. Fixed by 0837a (auto-assign series_number).
abbreviation is Column(String(10), nullable=False). Both columns always provided. Tenant-scoped. No NULL lockout risk.

name is nullable=False. version defaults to '1.0.0' (never NULL). product_id is nullable but standard PG treats NULLs as distinct in unique constraints by default (no NULLS NOT DISTINCT). Two templates with NULL product_id and same name+version would NOT collide.
document_name is always provided on creation. product_id is FK, always set. No NULL risk.
Both columns nullable=False. Tenant-scoped. No lockout risk.
Both columns nullable=False. Tenant-scoped. No lockout risk.
sequence is Column(Integer, nullable=False). Auto-assigned. product_id always provided. No NULL lockout risk.
Partial unique index enforces exactly one active product per tenant. This is intentional business logic — not a lockout bug. tenant_key is nullable=False.
Partial unique index enforces one active project per product. Intentional business logic. product_id always set.
Both are FK columns, nullable=False. Always provided. No NULL lockout risk.
Both columns always provided (FK + IP string). No NULL risk.
project_id is FK, path_key is always provided. No NULL lockout risk.
commit_hash is nullable=False. Globally unique by nature (SHA). No tenant scoping needed — git hashes are globally unique.
One setup state row per tenant. Intentional — tracks installation state. nullable=False.
Token is generated via UUID default, nullable=False. No lockout risk.
One cumulative metrics row per tenant (aggregate counters). Intentional singleton pattern. nullable=False.
Authorization code is always generated, nullable=False. No lockout risk.
Generated via UUID default, nullable=False. Globally unique by nature.

slug is unique globally, not per-tenant. This is likely by design (slugs used in URLs), but means two tenants cannot have orgs with the same slug. Low risk in CE (single tenant), potential SaaS consideration.
username is globally unique, not per-tenant. By design for auth (login by username). Low risk in CE. SaaS may need tenant-scoped usernames if multi-org.
email is nullable=True, unique=True. Standard PG allows multiple NULLs in unique columns (NULLS DISTINCT by default). Users without email don't collide.
key_hash is nullable=False, generated from bcrypt. Globally unique by nature.
Generated via UUID default, nullable=False. No lockout risk.
alias is nullable=False, generated via generate_project_alias() (random 6-char). Not tenant-scoped but collision probability is negligible (36^6 = 2.1B combinations). No NULL risk.
One git config per product. Intentional singleton pattern. product_id is FK, always set.

0837d.notes: Audited 24 unique constraints/indexes across all models. Only uq_project_taxonomy has the NULLS NOT DISTINCT lockout bug (fixed by 0837a). Two constraints (Organization.slug, User.username) are globally unique rather than tenant-scoped — this is by design for CE but flagged as SaaS considerations. No other constraints have NULL lockout risk. No missing tenant_key scoping issues beyond the noted global identifiers.

cross_cutting_findings.org_slug_global_uniqueness: Organization.slug is unique globally, not per-tenant. Fine for CE but SaaS multi-tenant may need tenant-scoped slugs. Low priority — not a lockout bug.
cross_cutting_findings.user_username_global_uniqueness: User.username is unique globally, not per-tenant. Standard for single-tenant auth but SaaS with multiple orgs may need scoping. Low priority.
